# Quality Gates

No video ships unless it passes these gates.

## Gate QG1 — Classification Gate

The viewer and platform must know what the video is about.

Pass criteria:

```text
one precise topic
spoken hook names the topic
caption/title names the topic
visuals show the topic
hashtags are relevant, not generic spam
```

Fail symptoms:

```text
low search traffic
wrong audience comments
high swipe-away despite good edit
```

## Gate QG2 — First-Frame Gate

The first frame must be understandable without sound.

Pass criteria:

```text
one dominant subject
problem/result visible
text readable on mobile
no visual clutter
no dead opening frame
```

## Gate QG3 — Hook Gate

The hook must create specific tension.

Pass criteria:

```text
names the viewer/problem
contains tension, payoff, or consequence
lands in 1–3 seconds
matches the body
```

Reject hooks like:

```text
“Here are some tips…”
“You won’t believe…”
“Watch until the end…”
```

## Gate QG4 — Payoff Gate

The first reward must arrive early.

Pass criteria:

```text
first useful/visual payoff by 3–7 seconds for most short-form videos
```

## Gate QG5 — Text/Cognition Gate

Text must reduce cognitive load.

Pass criteria:

```text
captions corrected
text not covering subject or UI
no huge central overlay over dynamic footage unless deliberately tested
labels used for comparisons
annotations used for small details
```

## Gate QG6 — Proof Gate

The video must show, not merely claim.

Pass criteria:

```text
before/after
screen recording
retention graph
analytics screenshot
real comment
case example
live edit/process
```

## Gate QG7 — Action Gate

The CTA must match the natural action.

```text
save = checklist/template/framework
send = social recipient/identity/warning
comment = A/B choice or useful prompt
follow = series promise or repeated expertise
```

## Gate QG8 — Trust Gate

The video must not create a trust deficit.

Fail criteria:

```text
clickbait not fulfilled
exaggerated claim
unverified data
stolen/reposted feel
AI slop/template sameness
misleading thumbnail/cover
```
